meta.title: Spine Surgery Practice Software | Conservative Care Tracking & Surgical LOMN | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to spine surgery practice operations. Track 6-12 weeks of conservative care automatically. Generate fusion LOMNs in 5 minutes. Recover $400K/year in OR capacity. Bill post-op RTM.

hero.subhead: Payers deny spine surgery for insufficient conservative care documentation. Your patients did the PT, the injections, the medication trials — but it's scattered across referral notes and outside records. clinIQ tracks conservative care from day one, generates the LOMN, and gets your surgery approved on first submission.

sections.content:
Spine surgery has the highest prior authorization burden in medicine. Lumbar fusions run $80,000-150,000. Payers fight every one.

The denial rate for spine surgery on first submission runs 25-40% nationally. The most common reason: insufficient documentation of conservative care. Not that the patient didn't do conservative care — they almost always did. The problem is documentation.

Your patient was referred after failing conservative treatment with their PCP and pain management. They did 8 weeks of PT. They had three epidural injections. They tried NSAIDs, muscle relaxants, gabapentin. They did everything right.

But when your surgical coordinator submits for authorization, the PT notes are in a different system. The injection records are at the pain clinic. The medication history is buried in the PCP's chart. Your LOMN says 'patient failed conservative care' without the specifics that payers require.

Denied. Peer-to-peer requested. Patient waits another 3 weeks in pain.

The average spine surgery authorization takes 15-20 hours of staff time across submissions, appeals, and peer-to-peer calls. Multiply that by 10-20 surgical cases per month, and you have a full-time employee doing nothing but prior auth.

This is fixable. The conservative care happened — it just needs to be tracked from the beginning, not reconstructed at authorization time.

sections.h2: Conservative Care Documentation: Track It From Day One
sections.content:
Payers require 6-12 weeks of documented conservative care before approving fusion. The specific requirements vary by payer, but typically include:

Physical therapy: 6-8 weeks of active PT with documented progress (or lack thereof). Dates, duration, specific interventions, outcome measures.

Medication trials: NSAIDs (with duration and reason for discontinuation), muscle relaxants, neuropathic agents (gabapentin, pregabalin), possibly opioids with documentation of inadequate relief.

Injection therapy: Epidural steroid injections, facet injections, medial branch blocks, trigger point injections. Dates, levels treated, percent relief, duration of relief.

Activity modifications: Work restrictions, lifting limitations, home exercise program compliance.

The problem isn't that your patients haven't done this. It's that no one is tracking it in a way that's accessible when you need to submit for authorization.

**What clinIQ does for conservative care:**

From the first visit, clinIQ creates a conservative care timeline for each surgical candidate:

PT tracking: Every PT visit logged with date, duration, and progress note summary. Your coordinator doesn't call the PT clinic for records — the timeline is already built.

Injection series: ESI #1 on 1/15, 40% relief for 3 weeks. ESI #2 on 2/5, 30% relief for 2 weeks. ESI #3 on 2/26, minimal relief. The progression is documented automatically based on follow-up visits.

Medication trials: Meloxicam 15mg daily × 6 weeks, discontinued for GI side effects. Gabapentin titrated to 600mg TID × 8 weeks, inadequate relief. Cyclobenzaprine 10mg TID × 4 weeks, minimal benefit. Each trial with start date, duration, max dose, and reason for discontinuation.

Activity modifications: Work restrictions documented. Home exercise compliance tracked at each visit.

When the surgeon decides to proceed with surgery, one click generates the complete conservative care summary. Six months of treatment, organized by category, with dates and outcomes. Attached to the LOMN. Submitted.

First-time approval rate goes from 60% to 92% because the documentation actually shows what happened.

sections.h2: Surgical LOMN Generation: 5 Minutes Instead of 45
sections.content:
The Letter of Medical Necessity for lumbar fusion is a 2-3 page document that takes 30-45 minutes to write manually. Your surgeon dictates. Your staff transcribes. Someone pulls imaging reports. Someone else chases down PT notes. Someone formats the letter. The surgeon reviews and signs.

For a busy spine practice doing 15 fusions per month, that's 8-12 hours per month on LOMN alone — assuming no denials requiring additional documentation.

**What a spine surgery LOMN requires:**

Patient demographics and procedure requested: ALIF L5-S1, PLIF L4-L5, laminectomy with fusion, etc.

Clinical presentation: Duration of symptoms, location of pain, radicular pattern, neurological deficits, functional limitations.

Physical examination findings: Motor weakness, sensory deficits, reflex changes, provocative testing, gait assessment.

Imaging findings: MRI demonstrating disc herniation, stenosis, spondylolisthesis, or degenerative changes at specific levels. Correlation between imaging and clinical presentation.

Conservative care history: The complete timeline of non-surgical treatment with dates, duration, and outcomes.

Medical necessity statement: Why this patient needs surgery now, why conservative care has failed, why the proposed procedure is appropriate.

**What clinIQ does for LOMN generation:**

Template library: Pre-built templates for ALIF, PLIF, TLIF, laminectomy, discectomy, cervical fusion, artificial disc replacement. Each template structured to meet payer-specific requirements for major insurers.

Auto-population: Patient demographics pulled from registration. Diagnosis codes from chart. Imaging findings from uploaded reports. Conservative care timeline from tracking system. Procedure details from surgical scheduling.

Surgeon workflow: Template loads with all available data populated. Surgeon reviews, adds clinical judgment and specific findings, edits as needed, signs electronically.

Time: 5 minutes for a complete LOMN instead of 45.

**The LOMN template library includes:**

Lumbar procedures: ALIF, PLIF, TLIF, lateral interbody fusion, laminectomy, discectomy, revision fusion

Cervical procedures: ACDF, posterior cervical fusion, cervical disc replacement, laminoplasty

Thoracic and complex: Thoracic fusion, deformity correction, tumor resection, trauma

Each template includes payer-specific language variations for Medicare, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, Blue Cross, and regional payers.

Spine surgery cases are long. A single-level lumbar fusion takes 2-3 hours. Complex cases take 4-6 hours. Your OR block time is fixed — you get 8 hours, maybe 10. The number of cases you do depends on two things: case duration and turnover time.

Case duration is mostly fixed based on surgical technique and patient complexity. Turnover time is variable — and most spine practices have never measured it.

**The turnover math:**

Typical OR day with 3 spine cases:

At 45-minute turnover between cases:
3 cases + 2 turnovers × 45 minutes = 90 minutes of turnover

At 25-minute turnover:
3 cases + 2 turnovers × 25 minutes = 50 minutes of turnover

Difference: 40 minutes — potentially enough for an additional short case or reduced staff overtime.

Over a year with 200 OR days, recovering 30-40 minutes per day equals 100-130 hours of OR capacity. At $3,000-4,000 per OR hour, that's $300,000-500,000 in recoverable capacity.

**Where spine OR turnover time goes:**

Room turnover (irreducible): 10-15 minutes for cleaning, re-draping, equipment setup for next case.

Patient positioning (reducible): 10-15 minutes for prone positioning, Wilson frame, fluoroscopy setup. Reducible with better coordination.

Waiting for next patient (highly reducible): 10-20 minutes because pre-op didn't know the room was ready, or the patient wasn't fully prepped, or anesthesia wasn't aware.

Equipment delays (eliminable): 5-15 minutes waiting for implants, neuromonitoring setup, cell saver, special equipment that should have been ready.

**What clinIQ does for OR efficiency:**

Room status visibility: Current case status (estimated 45 minutes remaining), turnover status, ready for next patient. Visible to OR, pre-op, surgeon, and anesthesia.

Pre-op synchronization: Next patient checked in, consent signed, anesthesia evaluation complete, positioned and ready — status visible before room is available.

Equipment verification: Implants confirmed, neuromonitoring tech present, special equipment staged — part of pre-case checklist.

Turnover tracking: Automatic timestamps for case end, room ready, next patient in. Analytics by day, by room, by team. You see where time goes and can address specific bottlenecks.

First case on-time starts: The 7:30 AM case that actually starts at 7:30 AM. First case delays cascade through the entire day — clinIQ tracks on-time start rates and identifies why delays happen.

Patient arrives for surgery. Medical clearance letter is missing. Labs are expired. The MRI is more than 6 months old and payer requires recent imaging. Implants weren't confirmed. Neuromonitoring wasn't scheduled.

The case is delayed or canceled. The patient is devastated. The OR slot is wasted.

**The pre-op checklist for spine surgery:**

Medical clearance: Cardiac clearance for patients with CAD, pulmonary clearance for COPD, diabetes optimization for HbA1c >8.

Labs: CBC, CMP, coagulation studies, type and screen. Results within payer-required timeframe (typically 30 days).

Imaging: MRI within 6 months (some payers require 3 months). CT myelogram if needed. Flexion-extension films for instability.

Prior authorization: Approved, auth number documented, valid through date confirmed.

Implants: Specific implants selected, ordered, confirmed available for surgery date.

Neuromonitoring: IONM company scheduled, tech confirmed, equipment availability verified.

Patient factors: NPO instructions given, ride arranged, post-op care plan in place, expectations set.

**What clinIQ does for pre-op coordination:**

Pre-op checklist with status tracking: Every item tracked with status (complete, pending, not started) and responsible party. Visible 14 days out, 7 days out, 2 days out.

Automated alerts: 'Medical clearance not received, surgery in 5 days' triggers alert to coordinator. 'Labs expiring before surgery date' surfaces automatically.

Implant tracking: Implants ordered, confirmed, received at facility — tracked status eliminates day-of surprises.

Patient communication: Automated pre-op instructions, NPO reminders, arrival time confirmation.

**The goal:** Every surgical patient arrives with everything complete. No canceled cases from missing items. No scrambling on the morning of surgery.

Your spine surgery patients have 6-12 week recoveries. They're supposed to follow activity restrictions, report concerning symptoms, track their progress. You want to know about wound issues, neurological changes, or worsening pain before they become emergencies.

This is Remote Therapeutic Monitoring. CPT 98980, 98981.

**What qualifies as post-op RTM for spine surgery:**

Pain tracking: Daily or weekly pain scores during recovery. Trending improvement versus plateau versus worsening.

Wound monitoring: Patient photos of incision. Signs of infection flagged automatically.

Neurological status: New or worsening numbness, weakness, bowel/bladder changes — red flags that need immediate attention.

Activity compliance: Following lifting restrictions, wearing brace if prescribed, progressing activity appropriately.

Medication management: Pain medication use, weaning progress, side effect reporting.

**The billing math:**

CPT 98980: RTM treatment management, first 20 minutes per month. ~$50-70.
CPT 98981: Each additional 20 minutes (if needed).

Combined with device supply codes (98975, 98977): $100-130 per patient per month.

Typical spine surgery recovery: 3 months of monitoring.

30 surgical patients × $120/month × 3 months = $10,800/quarter in RTM revenue.

This is revenue for work you're already doing — checking on post-op patients, fielding calls about symptoms, monitoring recovery. clinIQ documents it and makes it billable.

**What clinIQ does for post-op RTM:**

Patient app: Post-op spine surgery patients get a simple app for daily check-ins. Pain scores, wound photos, symptom reporting.

Alert thresholds: Fever >101? New neurological symptoms? Wound redness or drainage? Automatic alert to your team.

Provider dashboard: Which post-op patients are recovering normally? Which need attention? Which need to come in?

Automatic time tracking: Every interaction documented toward the 20-minute threshold. No manual time logs.

Billing integration: Monthly RTM billing generated automatically for eligible patients.

Spine surgery practices have complex workflows spanning clinic, pre-op coordination, OR, and post-op care. Implementation needs to account for that complexity.

**Week 1: Discovery and Configuration**

We map your complete surgical workflow: Initial consultation, conservative care tracking, surgical decision, pre-op coordination, surgery day, post-op visits. Each stage gets visibility.

We configure LOMN templates for your most common procedures and payers. We set up conservative care tracking categories based on your referral patterns.

**Week 2: Training**

Surgical coordinators learn pre-auth workflow and LOMN generation. Clinic staff learn conservative care tracking. Pre-op coordinators learn the surgical checklist. OR staff learn turnover tracking (if applicable).

Most staff are comfortable within 2-3 hours of role-specific training.

**Week 3: Go-Live**

Live operation with clinIQ team available for questions. New surgical candidates start on conservative care tracking. Pre-op checklists run for upcoming cases. LOMN generation begins.

**Week 4 and Beyond**

First LOMN generated in 5 minutes instead of 45. First surgery approved on first submission with complete conservative care documentation. Post-op RTM enrollment begins.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: LOMN time savings immediate (20+ hours/month recovered). First cases with clean conservative care documentation submitting.

Month 3: Approval rate improvement measurable. Pre-op cancellation rate decreasing. Post-op RTM generating revenue.

Month 6: Steady state operations. 90%+ first-submission approval rate. $10,000+/month in combined RTM revenue and recovered capacity.

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month — Full suite including LOMN generation, conservative care tracking, pre-op coordination, RTM

Implementation: $750 one-time — Workflow mapping, template customization, training

EHR Integration: Custom quote based on EHR and scope

faqs.q: How does conservative care tracking work?
faqs.a: Every PT visit, injection, medication trial logged with dates and outcomes from the first visit. When surgery is indicated, one click generates the complete conservative care timeline for the LOMN. No chasing records from outside providers — the timeline builds automatically based on patient visits and logged external care.

faqs.q: Do you generate letters of medical necessity?
faqs.a: Yes. Templates for ALIF, PLIF, TLIF, laminectomy, cervical fusion, and other procedures. Patient data, imaging findings, and conservative care history auto-populate. Surgeon reviews, edits, signs. 5 minutes instead of 45 minutes.

faqs.q: Which payers do your LOMN templates support?
faqs.a: Medicare, UnitedHealthcare, Aetna, Cigna, Blue Cross Blue Shield, Humana, and major regional payers. Each template includes payer-specific language and documentation requirements.

faqs.q: Can we track OR turnover time?
faqs.a: Yes. Case end, room ready, next patient in — all timestamped automatically. Trends by day, by room, by team. You see where time goes and can address specific bottlenecks.

faqs.q: Is post-op monitoring billable as RTM?
faqs.a: Yes. CPT 98980/98981. Patient-reported symptoms, wound photos, activity compliance, neurological status — reviewed by your team. Billable monthly during the recovery period, typically 3 months post-surgery.

faqs.q: What about complex cases requiring neuromonitoring?
faqs.a: Pre-op checklist includes neuromonitoring scheduling confirmation, tech availability, and equipment verification. No day-of surprises about missing equipment or unavailable techs.

faqs.q: Does this integrate with our surgical scheduling system?
faqs.a: We integrate with major surgical scheduling and EHR systems. Pre-op checklists sync with scheduled surgery dates. Conservative care timelines link to patient records.

faqs.q: How long until we see ROI?
faqs.a: LOMN time savings are immediate — 20+ hours/month recovered in the first month. Approval rate improvement typically visible by month 2-3. Post-op RTM revenue starts in month 1 for patients already in recovery.

cta.headline: Get your surgeries approved faster
cta.subhead: 15-minute demo. Conservative care tracking, LOMN generation, pre-op coordination, and post-op RTM configured for spine surgery.

testimonial.quote: We were getting denied for 'insufficient conservative care documentation' on patients who had done everything. Now we document it from day one. Approvals went from 60% to 92%.
